negative_variants.description: Branch Current Probe Contribution: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Contribute current through the named branch.

Branch Current Probe Contribution: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use a named branch with current contribution and current probing fail.
Branch Current Probe Contribution: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Branch Current Probe Contribution: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Contribute current through the named branch fails.

Branch Current Probe Contribution: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use a named branch with current contribution and current probing fail.
